channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Global Trends and Challenges in Economy, Politics, and Society
item.description: The article provides a detailed examination of contemporary global issues, focusing on three key areas: economic development, political dynamics, and societal challenges. It highlights the interconnec...

item.title: Twins' Commonwealth Games Journey Inspired by Mother's Past Struggles
item.description: Georgia and Olivia Nel, twin South African swimmers, are set to compete in the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, marking a significant milestone for their family. Their participation is deeply tied ...

item.title: England clinches World Cup bronze with Saka's hat-trick; Mbappé sets new scoring benchmark
item.description: The article details a pivotal moment in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where England secured third place after a thrilling match against France. Kylian Mbappé made history by becoming the first player to re...

item.title: Raiders hooker Owen Pattie facing suspension as Canberra's finals hopes suffer another setback
item.description: The Canberra Raiders have suffered another significant setback in their push for an NRL finals berth, with hooker Owen Pattie facing suspension following a Match Review Committee charge. Pattie was ci...

item.title: Summer Book Recommendations from Renowned Authors
item.description: The Independent has compiled a list of summer reading recommendations from acclaimed authors, offering a diverse range of genres and styles. Contributors include Helen Fielding, who suggests Joan Didi...

item.title: Zendaya and Elliot Page's casting in The Odyssey sparks controversy over 'woke' casting practices
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding the casting of Zendaya and Elliot Page in the film adaptation of 'The Odyssey,' with critics accusing the casting directors of prioritizing 'woke' age...

item.title: Iran attacks U.S. base in Jordan, killing two troops and injuring several
item.description: The U.S. military confirmed on July 18, 2026, that two troops were killed and one is missing following an Iranian attack on a base in Jordan. The strike, which occurred days after escalating clashes, ...

item.title: Private Sector Demands Review of Controversial Tourism Laws to Resolve Industry Conflicts
item.description: The Nigerian tourism industry is facing regulatory challenges due to overlapping laws from the NIHOTOUR Act and NTDA Act, which have created confusion and disputes among private sector players. The Fe...

item.title: manipur ethnic clashes: six naga villagers killed, two more arrested in kangpokpi district
item.description: In manipur's kangpokpi district, six naga villagers were abducted on may 13 and their bodies found on june 10, sparking protests by naga and meitei groups. police arrested two more suspects on saturda...

item.title: Facebook and Instagram Outages Reported by Users
item.description: The article reports that Facebook and Instagram are experiencing outages, as indicated by real-time monitoring service Downdetector. Both platforms, owned by Meta, are facing technical issues affectin...

item.title: Lies of P Complete Edition Receives Switch 2 Previews and Release Details
item.description: The release of Lies of P: Complete Edition on the Nintendo Switch 2 eShop next month has generated multiple previews highlighting both its strengths and areas for improvement. A day one patch is plann...

item.title: Tom Dearden scores try on return from injury for Cowboys
item.description: Tom Dearden has marked his return to NRL action with a crucial try for the North Queensland Cowboys, securing their opening score in the match. The 28-year-old halfback, who had been sidelined due to ...

item.title: NCCIA summons Imran's sister over alleged false content dissemination
item.description: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan, to appear before it on July 20 over allegations of spreading false, offensi...

item.title: Tate Brothers Face UK Legal Charges and Extradition Request
item.description: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested by US Marshals in Miami on Saturday, days before Andrew was set to co-host a bareknuckle boxing event. The UK's Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) has ch...

item.title: EJ Obiena Sets Silver Medal in Madrid, Eyes Asian Games
item.description: Filipino pole vaulter EJ Obiena has made a significant comeback after nearly two years of consistent performance. At the Meeting Madrid in Spain on July 16, he cleared 5.85 meters to secure silver, ma...

item.title: Tanzania and China Expand Economic Cooperation in Trade and Technology
item.description: The article discusses recent developments in Tanzanian politics and international relations. It highlights Tanzania's growing economic ties with China, focusing on collaboration in trade, industrializ...

item.title: Systemic Food Safety Concerns as Foreign Nationals Operate Spaza Shops in Gauteng
item.description: A report by the Public Protector reveals systemic failures in Gauteng's food safety regulations, highlighting that foreign nationals operate approximately 56% of spaza shops in the region. The investi...

item.title: Horoscope for July 19, 2026: Insights for Each Zodiac Sign
item.description: This article provides horoscope predictions for July 19, 2026, tailored to each zodiac sign. Aries (March 21–April 20) is advised to distance themselves from critical individuals and embrace new oppor...

item.title: Avocados May Help Lower Cholesterol and Reduce Heart Disease Risk
item.description: A new study published in the Journal of Clinical Lipidology suggests that consuming avocados daily could significantly lower 'bad' cholesterol levels and reduce heart disease risk. The research, condu...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler's memorial service in Wales as fans pay tribute to late singer
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh singer known for her iconic 1980s hits, has passed away at the age of 75. Her family has announced that a memorial service will be held in her hometown of Rhyl, Wales, where fa...
